Registry / Terms / maximum permissible error (mpe)

maximum permissible error (mpe)

VIMOutdated — VIM 1993 G 18 #00826 · 17 instances

Authoritative definition

VIM 1993 · concept #5.21view ↗

extreme values of an error permitted by specifications, regulations, etc. for a given measuring instrument

Outdated: Cites VIM 1993. Latest: VIM 2012.
  • VIM 1993 · #5.21
    extreme values of an error permitted by specifications, regulations, etc. for a given measuring instrument

Suggested actions

  1. HIGH ✗ This term is NOT in VIM 2012.
  2. MEDIUM 16 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

16 distinct definitions across 17 publications. 1 definitions are shared by multiple publications (merge candidates).

2 identical2010202X
maximum value of the error permitted by laws, regulations, or specifications for a given measuring instrument
Ed.YearPublicationClauseG 18 #Consistency

[a extreme values of the error (positive or negative) permitted by this Recommendation dapted from VIM:1993, 5.21].
pending

pending
